info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Worker Journeys/Tasks
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.

parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Tasks resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Tasks. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Tasks collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Tasks to get the hash key.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • AuthenticationFormat; string; Password authentication format for an electronic signature task.
  • JourneyTaskId; integer; Identifier of the journey task.
  • LearningContentTypeMeaning; string; Meaning of the learning content type.
  • Name; string; Name of the journey task.
  • PerformerPersonId; integer; Person identifier of the task performer. Available only in case of single performer, is updatable when adding a new task.
  • ProcessingMode; string; Processing mode to send journey notifications.
  • RequiredFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the task is mandatory or optional. Valid values are Yes and No. The default value is No.
  • SaveAttachmentsToDORForMeaning; string; Meaning of save attachments to document records.
  • Status; string; Status code of the task.
  • TargetDurationUOMMeaning; string; Meaning of unit of measure used in the task target duration.
  • VideoTypeMeaning; string; Meaning of video type.
  • WorkerJourneyTaskId; integer; Unique identifier of the journey task.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds all worker journey tasks that match the primary key specified.
    Finder Variables
    • WorkerJourneyTaskId; integer; Unique identifier of the journey task.
  • findActivePhases Finds all active journey phases.
    Finder Variables
    • findAvailableSelfTasks Finds all journey tasks except deferred tasks for the signed in user.
      Finder Variables
      • findOthersTasks Finds all journey tasks for users other than the signed in user and journey assignee.
        Finder Variables
        • findSelfTasks Finds all journey tasks for the signed in user.
          Finder Variables
          • findWorkerTasks Finds all journey tasks for the journey assignee.
            Finder Variables

parameters.name: Upsert-Mode
parameters.description: Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, the server performs an Upsert operation instead of a Create operation. During an Upsert operation, the server attempts to find an existing resource that matches the payload. If a match is found, the server updates the existing resource instead of creating a new one. If not found or false (default), the server performs a Create operation. Note that the Upsert operation isn't supported for date-effective REST resources.

DisabledForOthersFlag.description: Indicates if the journey template task property is disabled for other users. Default value depends on the task type.

DisabledForOwnerFlag.description: Indicates if the journey template task property is disabled for owner. Default value depends on the task type.

DisabledForPerformerFlag.description: Indicates if the journey template task property is disabled for performer. Default value depends on the task type.

LineManagerLookupType.description: Journey template task property for performer. Lookup type of the journey template task property for line manager.

QuestionnaireParticipantId.title: Questionnaire Participant Id
QuestionnaireParticipantId.description: Surrogate identifier for the questionnaire participant.

QuestionnaireResponseId.title: Questionnaire Response Id
QuestionnaireResponseId.description: Surrogate identifier for the questionnaire response.

QuestionnaireVersionNumber.title: Questionnaire Version
QuestionnaireVersionNumber.description: Version number of the questionnaire that the participant responds to.

Status.title: Response Status Code
Status.description: Status of the questionnaire response. I denotes the response is in progress and not submitted yet, while S denotes a submitted response.

questionResponses.title: Question Responses
questionResponses.description: The questionResponses resource is a child of the questionnaireResponses resource. It includes responses to specific questions within a questionnaire.

AnswerCodeList.title: Answer Code List
AnswerCodeList.description: Concatenated list of answer choices, separated by a semicolon as a delimiter, which is selected by the participant when responding to a multiple choice question.

AnswerComments.title: Comments
AnswerComments.description: Additional comments provided by the participant when responding to a question.

AnswerLargeObject.title: Answer
AnswerLargeObject.description: Attachment provided by the participant when responding to a question.

AnswerList.title: Answer List
AnswerList.description: Concatenated list of answer identifiers, separated by a semicolon as a delimiter, which is selected by the participant when responding to a multiple choice question.

ChoiceCodeList.title: Choice List Code
ChoiceCodeList.description: Concatenated list of answer codes, separated by a semicolon as a delimiter, which are presented to the participant when responding to a question enabled for random order responses.

ChoiceList.title: Choice List
ChoiceList.description: Concatenated list of answer identifiers, separated by a semicolon as a delimiter, which are presented to the participant when responding to a question enabled for random order responses.

QuestionAnswerId.title: Question Answer Id
QuestionAnswerId.description: Surrogate identifier of the answer that a participant chose when responding to a single choice question.

QuestionCode.title: Question Code
QuestionCode.description: Alphanumeric value that uniquely identifies a question defined for a subscriber.

QuestionResponseId.title: Question Response Id
QuestionResponseId.description: Surrogate identifier for the question response.

QuestionnaireQuestionId.title: Questionnaire Question Id
QuestionnaireQuestionId.description: Surrogate identifier for the question in a questionnaire.

questionResponseAttachments.title: Question Response Attachments
questionResponseAttachments.description: The questionResponseAttachments resource is a child of the questionResponses resource. It includes attachments added in response to a question.

AsyncTrackerId.title: Asynchronous Upload Tracker Id
AsyncTrackerId.description: Attribute provided exclusively for the Attachment UI components to assist in uploading files.

AttachedDocumentId.title: Attached Document Id
AttachedDocumentId.description: Unique identifier of the attached document.

CategoryName.title: Category
CategoryName.description: Category name of the attachment. This is a read-only attribute.

ContentRepositoryFileShared.title: Shared
ContentRepositoryFileShared.description: Indicates whether the file can be shared. The default value is false and it's the only supported value.

DatatypeCode.title: Type
DatatypeCode.description: Attachment type supported for the question. Only the attachment type, FILE, is supported.

DmDocumentId.description: Surrogate identifier of the document attached to the questionnaire.

DownloadInfo.title: DownloadInfo
DownloadInfo.description: JSON object represented as a string containing information used to programmatically retrieve a file attachment.

ErrorStatusCode.title: Error Status Code
ErrorStatusCode.description: Error status code of the attachment.

ErrorStatusMessage.title: Error Status Message
ErrorStatusMessage.description: Error status message of the attachment.

ExpirationDate.title: Expiration Date
ExpirationDate.description: Expiration date of the attachment. This is a read-only attribute.

PostProcessingAction.title: PostProcessingAction
PostProcessingAction.description: The name of the action that can be performed after an attachment is uploaded.

UploadedFileContentType.title: Uploaded File Content Type
UploadedFileContentType.description: Content type of the uploaded file. This is a read-only attribute.

UploadedFileLength.title: File Size
UploadedFileLength.description: Size of the uploaded file. This is a read-only attribute.

UploadedFileName.title: Uploaded File Name
UploadedFileName.description: Name of the uploaded file. This is a read-only attribute.

Uri.title: Uri
Uri.description: URI of the attachment. This is a read-only attribute.

Url.title: Url
Url.description: URL of a web page attachment.

Metadata-Context.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

REST-Framework-Version.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.
